% Dual-majority data macros: C++ majority vs.\ c-havoc_std majority (1010 shared tasks).
% Needs \usepackage[table]{xcolor} and \usepackage{pgfplotstable}.
% Regenerated from analysis.ipynb -- do not edit. \input once, then cite \dualCHavocStd... inline.
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTrueTrue}{115}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTrueFalse}{446}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTrueTie}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTrueUnknown}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTrueTotal}{561}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdFalseTrue}{7}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdFalseFalse}{397}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdFalseTie}{2}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdFalseUnknown}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdFalseTotal}{406}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTieTrue}{3}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTieFalse}{27}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTieTie}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTieUnknown}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTieTotal}{30}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdUnknownTrue}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdUnknownFalse}{12}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdUnknownTie}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdUnknownUnknown}{1}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdUnknownTotal}{13}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTotalTrue}{125}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTotalFalse}{882}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTotalTie}{2}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTotalUnknown}{1}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdTotal}{1010}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdAgree}{513}
\newcommand{\dualCHavocStdDisagree}{497}

\ifdefined\dualcolorcells\else\let\dualcolorcells\relax
  % Square data cells: each cell is an explicit \colorbox of side \dualcellw -- a
  % fixed-width \makebox with a strut of the same height, no \fboxsep and no column
  % padding (@{}c@{}), so the shaded boxes are equal squares and tile contiguously.
  % \dualcellw auto-sizes to the widest header so nothing wraps.
  \newlength{\dualcellw}\settowidth{\dualcellw}{unknown}\addtolength{\dualcellw}{0.9em}%
  \newcommand{\dualbox}[2]{{\setlength{\fboxsep}{0pt}%
    \colorbox{black!#1}{\makebox[\dualcellw][c]{%
      \rule[-0.5\dualcellw]{0pt}{\dualcellw}\ifnum#1>50 \color{white}\fi #2}}}}%
  \pgfplotstableset{
    color cells/.style={
      col sep=comma, string type, column type={@{}c@{}},
      % Shade each count on the black! scale, normalised to the per-table max \heatVmax
      % (so the printed value is the raw count but the colour is 0..100). The shade is
      % computed here, at parse time, and baked into \dualbox as a literal.
      postproc cell content/.code={%
        \pgfmathtruncatemacro\dualshade{round(100*(##1/\heatVmax))}%
        \pgfkeysalso{@cell content/.expanded={\noexpand\dualbox{\dualshade}{\noexpand##1}}}%
      },
      columns/x/.style={column name={}, column type={r@{\hskip 0.6em}}, postproc cell content/.code={}},
    },
  }
\fi

\begin{table}[t]
  \centering
  \caption{Dual majority over the 1010 tasks shared by the original C++ suite and \texttt{c-havoc_std}: rows are the C++ majority verdict, columns the \texttt{c-havoc_std} majority verdict; cell shading is the count relative to the table maximum.}
  \label{tab:dual-majority-c-havoc_std-macros}
  \def\heatVmax{446}%
  \setlength{\extrarowheight}{0pt}\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{0}% rows tile: each \dualbox sets its own square height
  \pgfplotstabletypeset[color cells]{
x,true,false,tie,unknown
true,\dualCHavocStdTrueTrue,\dualCHavocStdTrueFalse,\dualCHavocStdTrueTie,\dualCHavocStdTrueUnknown
false,\dualCHavocStdFalseTrue,\dualCHavocStdFalseFalse,\dualCHavocStdFalseTie,\dualCHavocStdFalseUnknown
tie,\dualCHavocStdTieTrue,\dualCHavocStdTieFalse,\dualCHavocStdTieTie,\dualCHavocStdTieUnknown
unknown,\dualCHavocStdUnknownTrue,\dualCHavocStdUnknownFalse,\dualCHavocStdUnknownTie,\dualCHavocStdUnknownUnknown
  }
\end{table}
